Space layout: per floor: 3 units (1B/2B/2B) + elevator lobby + stair core
Description: A low-rise multifamily apartment building located in Guangzhou, China. The building footprint is approximately 21.2 m (east-west) by 14.1 m (north-south), rectangular, with the long axis rotated 225 degrees from north. Total gross floor area is about 900.0 m^2 spread over 3 floor(s), giving roughly 300.0 m^2 per floor. Floor-to-floor height is 3.0 m. The building is divided into 18 thermal zones in total (about 6 per floor). There are 3 dwelling units per floor (2 thermal zone(s) per unit), so model each unit as its own zone(s) stacked vertically across the 3 floor(s), plus a shared core/circulation. Typical space layout: per floor: 3 units (1B/2B/2B) + elevator lobby + stair core. Window-to-wall ratio is approximately 20% on exterior walls, with windows distributed on all four facades. Model all zones with appropriate constructions, glazing, occupancy, lighting, equipment and HVAC (ideal loads) so the IDF runs in EnergyPlus without errors.
